meta.description: Compute time-dependent AUC and ROC curves at multiple clinical time points for survival model evaluation. Inputs: survival time, event status, continuous biomarker or risk score. Outputs: time-AUC line plot, AUC table at 1/3/5 years, iAUC summary.

scientific_integrity.detail: Outputs are computed from supplied data and no fabricated study claims, identifiers, or effect estimates were introduced by the skill.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill computes ROC and AUC artifacts only and does not cross into diagnosis, treatment advice, or prescriptive clinical recommendations.

methodological_ground.detail: The workflow correctly targets time-dependent ROC analysis for survival data and rejects unusable time specifications before running analysis.

code_usability.detail: The R entry point executed successfully on valid inputs in this environment and produced the documented tables, figure, and session metadata.

functional_suitability.note: Core workflow, supported formats, default marker behavior, and outputs are covered well; advanced plotting and export flags are only partially documented in SKILL.md.

reliability.note: Validation paths and error prefixes are strong, but recovery guidance for dependency setup and rerun behavior could be more explicit.

performance_context.note: Progressive disclosure is handled well through a concise SKILL.md plus references and modular scripts.

agent_usability.note: The execution path is easy to follow, though agents still need `--help` to discover several advanced flags that appear in examples.

human_usability.note: Trigger language is natural enough for statistical-analysis requests, but the skill assumes some survival-analysis vocabulary.

security.note: Input validation is strong and no secrets are involved, but the documentation does not explicitly discuss PHI handling or de-identification expectations for patient-level data.

maintainability.note: Code is cleanly split across utility, validation, analysis, and plotting files, with bundled sample data for verification.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision and layering are strong; explicit stop conditions for unsupported interpretation requests and overwrite caveats would make the skill more robust.

inputs.label: Default risk_score on sample1
inputs.note: Executed cleanly and produced every documented artifact in the expected directory structure.

assertions.text: Output creates the documented AUC table in table/time_roc_auc.csv
assertions.note: File exists and contains the requested time points.

assertions.text: Output creates the documented ROC point export in data/time_roc_points.csv
assertions.note: ROC point file was generated with the expected columns.

assertions.text: Output creates both figure/time_roc.pdf and session_info.txt
assertions.note: Both reproducibility artifacts were written successfully.

assertions.text: Default marker handling uses risk_score when --marker_col is omitted
assertions.note: Execution log and AUC table both report risk_score.

inputs.label: Alternate marker GPR161 on sample2
inputs.note: The non-default marker path worked without adjustment and preserved the requested time points.

assertions.text: The alternate marker column is accepted and surfaced in outputs
assertions.note: marker_col is reported as GPR161 in the logs and exported table.

assertions.text: The requested time points are preserved in the output table
assertions.note: The AUC table contains rows for 1, 2, and 4 only.

assertions.text: The documented output structure is created successfully
assertions.note: data, table, figure, and session_info.txt are all present.

assertions.text: The workflow succeeds without manual intervention on a non-default marker
assertions.note: The run completed cleanly from the CLI alone.

inputs.label: Missing required data_file
inputs.note: This is an expected validation failure path; the skill rejected the request cleanly and printed help text.

assertions.text: Missing input is rejected with SKILL_MISSING_INPUT
assertions.note: The command exits immediately with the documented error prefix.

assertions.text: The failure path prints actionable recovery guidance
assertions.note: Full CLI help appears after the error message.

assertions.text: The process fails cleanly instead of hanging or emitting a stack trace
assertions.note: Output is controlled and human-readable.

assertions.text: No output directory or partial analysis artifacts are created on this path
assertions.note: No TimeROC_Results directory was created during the failed run.

inputs.label: Invalid non-positive times
inputs.note: This is an expected validation failure path; parameter checks ran before any data loading or analysis work.

assertions.text: Non-positive time points are rejected with SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER
assertions.note: The command fails with the expected validation message.

assertions.text: Help text is emitted after the validation failure
assertions.note: Recovery guidance is printed inline.

assertions.text: Validation happens before analysis execution begins
assertions.note: No data-loading or ROC analysis logs appear before the failure.

assertions.text: No output directory is created for the rejected run
assertions.note: tests/audit_eval/invalid_times was not created.

inputs.label: Five-timepoint TXT export on sample3
inputs.note: A higher-parameter run completed successfully, including TXT exports, figure generation, and session metadata.

assertions.text: TXT exports are produced in the documented data and table subdirectories
assertions.note: Both time_roc_points.txt and time_roc_auc.txt were written correctly.

assertions.text: The selected marker and weighting are preserved in the output table
assertions.note: The exported AUC table records RIBC2 and marginal.

assertions.text: All requested time points are carried through to the results
assertions.note: The table contains five rows for 1, 2, 3, 5, and 8.

assertions.text: The figure and session metadata are emitted for reproducibility
assertions.note: time_roc.pdf and session_info.txt both exist.

key_strengths: The skill cleanly separates concise user-facing instructions from implementation details in scripts and references.
CLI validation is strong: missing inputs and invalid parameters fail early with stable SKILL_* messages and help output.
The implementation is executable as shipped and produces reproducible table, figure, and session metadata artifacts on valid runs.
Marker-column flexibility, multiple file formats, and standardized output directories make the skill practical for repeated analysis tasks.

recommendations.title: Document advanced CLI options in SKILL.md
recommendations.problem: Several useful options exposed by the CLI, including export format, figure styling, and legend placement values, are only discoverable through --help or by reading scripts.
recommendations.root_cause: SKILL.md focuses on the core path and examples but does not summarize the broader parameter surface area.
recommendations.fix: Add a compact advanced-options table covering output format, figure customization, and accepted legend-position values. Keep the main workflow short and defer long detail to references.

recommendations.title: Add data-handling and interpretation boundaries
recommendations.problem: The documentation does not explicitly remind users to de-identify patient-level data or clarify that ROC/AUC outputs are analytical artifacts rather than clinical recommendations.
recommendations.root_cause: Operational and safety notes are currently centered on dependencies and file validation, not on real-world data governance or interpretation limits.
recommendations.fix: Add a short operational-notes section stating that inputs should be de-identified where required and that the skill reports discrimination metrics without making treatment or diagnostic decisions.

recommendations.title: Clarify rerun and auto-conversion behavior
recommendations.problem: The scripts will reuse the requested output directory and can overwrite prior artifacts, while automatic day-to-year or day-to-month conversion is only briefly described.
recommendations.root_cause: These behaviors exist in code and logs but are not called out prominently in the operator-facing workflow.
recommendations.fix: Document that rerunning with the same output directory overwrites matching filenames and explain when auto-conversion is triggered, including how to disable it with --auto_convert_days FALSE.
